name: Buttermilk Bread in a Pressure Cooker
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Buttermilk (You can make quite adequate buttermilk from dried milk)
4 cups Water for pressure cooker with rack.
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the egg and sugar.
2. Sift together the flour, soda, and baking powder.
3. Add the buttermilk and sifted dry ingredients alternately, mixing well.
4. Add nuts and dried fruit (nut, dates, raisins).
5. Pour into a buttered bowl and cover with wax paper.
6. Place the bowl in the pressure cooker on the rack with water.
7. Cover the cooker and steam without the pressure valve for 15 minutes.
8. Cook at high pressure for 25 minutes.
9. Allow to cool naturally.
This recipe works well and makes quite tasty bread. After a week at sea, we (a crew of four) could polish off a whole loaf at one sitting, especially if the butter was still firm.
